supportedSites.label: WordPress
notes: Use side-by-side deployment by default.
Do not replace the existing WordPress home page unless the owner explicitly selects root replacement.
Expose current site and user context through the plugin REST bridge for VoiceLink onboarding.

supportedSites.label: Composr CMS
notes: Treat /home/<user>/public_html as the active Composr root and preserve it by default.
If the real Composr root is nested under /home/<user>/public_html/*, detect that child root first instead of assuming the parent is the site root.
Deploy VoiceLink beside the CMS or through linked pages and custom sources, not by replacing the entire site root unless explicitly chosen.
Expose logged-in Composr member identity, email, and mapped VoiceLink role through a bridge endpoint so owned servers can attach to existing VoiceLink identities.
Use Composr page paths such as pages/*/*, pg/*/*, site/pages/*, and html_custom/html where needed so VoiceLink pages still render properly even on minimal Composr installs.
If Composr chat is installed, public VoiceLink rooms may map to Composr chat rooms; otherwise VoiceLink falls back to its own room chat module.
Import Composr groups, clubs, private topics, and direct-message relationships so private VoiceLink access can follow existing Composr membership rules.

supportedSites.label: WHMCS
notes: Treat /home/<user>/public_html or the detected WHMCS root as a billing and client portal integration target, not a disposable webroot.
Preserve the existing WHMCS site root by default and add VoiceLink through addon, template, hook, and client-area entry points.
Expose linked client, admin, owned-domain, and service identity context so VoiceLink can attach the install to an existing VoiceLink owner instead of creating a duplicate user.
Use WHMCS client, admin, service, and owned-domain records for VoiceLink account linking, licensing, owned-server detection, and notifications.

supportedSites.label: cPanel Account
notes: Treat the cPanel account as a managed hosting root that can expose file manager, database, backup, and domain-management capabilities to VoiceLink.
Prefer side-by-side deployment under apps or a dedicated web subpath instead of replacing the main site root.
Allow VoiceLink to reuse cPanel-owned home paths, domains, databases, and file-sharing directories when linking installs to the same owner identity.
Use cPanel File Manager style paths for VoiceLink shared files and admin upload actions when supported.

supportedSites.label: Installatron Application
notes: Treat Installatron as a deployment lifecycle and package-management layer around the existing VoiceLink browser UI.
Preserve the app/webroot by default and sync Installatron install metadata, .well-known domain binding, and WHMCS license status into the browser and desktop admin UI.
Expose app install status, license validation, and path binding through shared API routes so browser UI and desktop admin management stay aligned.
Keep ACME .well-known challenge files untouched while publishing /.well-known/voicelink.json for install discovery.
